atDirectives.name: @tailwind
atDirectives.description: Use the `@tailwind` directive to insert Tailwind's `base`, `components`, `utilities` and `screens` styles into your CSS.

atDirectives.name: @apply
atDirectives.description: Use `@apply` to inline any existing utility classes into your own custom CSS.

atDirectives.name: @layer
atDirectives.description: Use the `@layer` directive to tell Tailwind which “bucket” a set of custom styles belong to.

atDirectives.name: @config
atDirectives.description: Use the `@config` directive to load a legacy JavaScript config file.

atDirectives.name: @plugin
atDirectives.description: Use the `@plugin` directive to load a Tailwind CSS plugin.

atDirectives.name: @theme
atDirectives.description: Use the `@theme` directive to define design tokens for your project.

atDirectives.name: @source
atDirectives.description: Use the `@source` directive to explicitly specify source files that aren't picked up by Tailwind's automatic content detection.

atDirectives.name: @utility
atDirectives.description: Use the `@utility` directive to define custom utilities.

atDirectives.name: @variant
atDirectives.description: Use the `@variant` directive to apply a Tailwind variant in custom CSS.

atDirectives.name: @custom-variant
atDirectives.description: Use the `@custom-variant` directive to add a custom variant.

atDirectives.name: @reference
atDirectives.description: Use the `@reference` directive to import Tailwind theme variables without including CSS in the output.
